issues.description: The function uses uint8_t as the return type and for variables, which is acceptable if stdint.h types are the project standard. However, if the project typedefs require uint32_t or int32_t as the default integer type, this may be a violation. Please confirm project typedef policy.
issues.suggested_fix: If project guidelines require, change uint8_t to uint32_t or int32_t as appropriate.

issues.description: The variable 'rx' is declared but not explicitly initialized before use. Although HAL_UART_Receive will write to it on success, if HAL_UART_Receive fails, 'rx' remains uninitialized, which could be misleading.
issues.suggested_fix: Initialize 'rx' to 0 at declaration: uint8_t rx = 0U;

issues.description: The pointer parameter 'cmd_out' is used for output only. If it is not intended to be modified outside, consider using the const qualifier for read-only data. However, in this case, it is being written to, so this may not apply. If any input pointers are read-only, mark them as const.
issues.suggested_fix: No change needed for 'cmd_out', but review all pointer parameters for const correctness.

issues.description: The function uses hard-coded numeric constants such as '1', '10', '0', and '5'. These are magic numbers and should be replaced with named constants or macros for clarity and maintainability.
issues.suggested_fix: Define macros such as #define UART_RX_TIMEOUT_MS 10U and #define CMD_MIN '0', #define CMD_MAX '5', and use them in the function.

issues.description: The function lacks a descriptive header comment explaining its purpose, parameters, and return value.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a header comment describing what the function does, its parameters, and its return value.

issues.description: The function has multiple return statements, which can reduce maintainability and make debugging harder.
issues.suggested_fix: Refactor the function to use a single return statement at the end.

issues.description: The function uses if statements without braces, which can lead to errors if the code is modified in the future.
issues.suggested_fix: Add braces around all if statement bodies.

issues.description: The function does not check if the pointer 'cmd_out' is NULL before dereferencing it, which could lead to undefined behavior if a NULL pointer is passed.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a check to ensure 'cmd_out' is not NULL before dereferencing.

issues.description: The function does not include a descriptive header comment explaining its purpose, parameters, and return values.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a header comment at the beginning of the function describing its purpose, parameters, and return values.

issues.description: Hard coded numeric constants (such as 8, 336, 2, 7, 5, 4) are used directly in the function.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace magic numbers with named constants or macros to improve readability and maintainability.

issues.description: Configuration values for the PLL and clock dividers are not centralized; they are hardcoded in the function.
issues.suggested_fix: Move configuration values to a dedicated configuration header file and reference them using macros or constants.

issues.description: The function exceeds fifty executable lines, making it lengthy and harder to maintain or test.
issues.suggested_fix: Split the function into smaller, reusable functions such as separate functions for enabling clocks, configuring output pins, configuring input pins, and configuring alternate function pins.

issues.description: The function does not include a descriptive header comment explaining its purpose, parameters, and return values.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a header comment at the beginning of the function describing its purpose (GPIO initialization), parameters (none), and return value (none).

issues.description: Hard coded numeric constants such as GPIO_PIN_13, GPIO_PIN_RESET, GPIO_PIN_SET, GPIO_SPEED_FREQ_LOW, and alternate function values are used directly in the code.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace magic numbers with named constants or macros defined in a centralized configuration header if not already defined by the HAL library.

issues.description: Configuration values for GPIO initialization (such as pin numbers, modes, speeds, and alternate functions) are scattered throughout the function.
issues.suggested_fix: Centralize configuration values in a dedicated configuration header or structure to improve maintainability and consistency.

issues.description: The function lacks a descriptive header comment explaining its purpose, parameters, and return values.

issues.description: Configuration values (such as ADC prescaler, resolution, channel, and sample time) are hardcoded in the function body.

issues.description: Error conditions are handled by calling Error_Handler(), but no error logging or diagnostic information is provided.

issues.description: Hard coded numeric constants are used for BaudRate (115200).

issues.description: The function uses uint8_t for the parameter and local variable, but if the project standardizes on uint32_t or int32_t as fixed-width integer types, this may be a violation unless uint8_t is explicitly allowed by project typedefs.
